zh.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob Nagel是Jacob Nagel和Maria Margaretha的兒子，於1728年出生。他於1747年與Elisabetha Mohren在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 庫瑟爾岡格洛夫結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他埋葬在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 庫瑟爾岡格洛夫。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob Nagel是Jacob Nagel和Maria Margaretha的儿子，于1728年出生。他于1747年与Elisabetha Mohren在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 庫瑟爾岡格洛夫结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他埋葬在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 庫瑟爾岡格洛夫。

nameMeanings.name: Nagel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Heinz, Hans, Otto, Kurt, Armin, Franz, Fritz, Udo, Wilhelm, Aloys.
German, Dutch, Scandinavian, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German, Middle Dutch nagel, German Nagel ‘nail’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of nails. Compare Nail .
Germanized form of Sorbian Nahł, Nagł, Nahły, and Nagły: nickname from Upper Sorbian nahły, Lower Sorbian nagły ‘quick’, also ‘hot-tempered’.

variants.name: Nogle
meanings: Possibly an altered form of German   Nagel   1.

variants.name: Nail
meanings: English: from Middle English naile, nayle ‘nail’ (from Old English nægel), which may, as in modern English, have the senses ‘metal spike, wooden peg’or ‘fingernail, toenail’. Applied as a metonymic occupational name for a maker of nails.
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Nagel   ‘nail’.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Nahilo, based on the element nah ‘sufficient, adequate’.

variants.name: Naegele
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Alois, Bernhard, Erwin, Heinz, Hilde, Manfred, Matthias, Wolfgang.
South German (Nägele): variant of Nagel . Compare Nagele .

variants.name: Naegeli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Markus, Otto, Wolfgang.

variants.name: Negley
meanings: Americanized form of Swiss German Naegeli or Nägeli, variants of Nagel . Compare Nagley .

variants.name: Nagele
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Jutta, Erwin, Rainer, Siegfried.
South German (Nägele): variant of Nagel . Compare Naegele .

variants.name: Noggle
meanings: Possibly an altered form of German Nöggel, a variant of Nöckel, a South German topographic name from Nock ‘hill, peak, rock’, or of Nagel 1. See Nock .

nameMeanings.name: Johann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Jacob
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, from Hebrew Yaakov. This was borne by perhaps the most important of all the patriarchs in the Book of Genesis. Jacob was the father of twelve sons, who gave their names to the twelve tribes of Israel. He was the son of Isaac and Rebecca and twin brother of Esau. According to the story in Genesis, he was the cunning younger twin, who persuaded his brother Esau to part with his right to his inheritance in exchange for a bowl of soup (‘a mess of pottage’). Later, he tricked his blind and dying father into blessing him in place of Esau. The derivation of the name has been much discussed. It is traditionally explained as being derived from Hebrew akev ‘heel’ and to have meant ‘heel grabber’, because when Jacob was born ‘his hand took hold of Esau's heel’ (Genesis 25:26). This is interpreted later in the Bible as ‘supplanter’ Esau himself remarks, ‘Is he not rightly named Jacob? for he has supplanted me these two times’ (Genesis 27:36). Jacob is especially common as a Jewish given name, although it also became very popular among the Puritans from the 16th century onwards, and has again been widely used since the 1990s. Compare James .

en.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob Nagel was born in 1728 as the son of Jacob Nagel and Maria Margaretha. He married Elisabetha Mohren in 1747, in Gangloff, Kusel, Bavaria, Germany. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He was buried in Gangloff, Kusel, Bavaria, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob Nagel est né en 1728, fils de Jacob Nagel et Maria Margaretha. Il a épousé Elisabetha Mohren en 1747, à Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Ilenterré à Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob Nagel wurde 1728, als Sohn von Jacob Nagel und Maria Margaretha geboren. Er hat 1747 in Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Deutschland Elisabetha Mohren geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er wurde in Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob Nagel è nato il nel 1728 come figlio di Jacob Nagel e Maria Margaretha. Lui sposato Elisabetha Mohren nel 1747, nel Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Fu sepolto a Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob Nagelは1728年にJacob NagelとMaria Margarethaの息子として生まれた。1747年にドイツバイエルンランドクレイス クセルガングロッフでElisabetha Mohrenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼はドイツバイエルンランドクレイス クセルガングロッフにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob Nagel(은)는 1728년 Jacob Nagel(와)과 Maria Margaretha의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1747년 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 쿠셀 갱글로프에서 Elisabetha Mohren(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 쿠셀 갱글로프에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob Nagel nasceu em 1728 filho de Jacob Nagel e Maria Margaretha. Ele casou-se com Elisabetha Mohren em 1747, em Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele foi sepultado em Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob Nagel родился в 1728 г. Его родителей звали Jacob Nagel и Maria Margaretha. Он женился на женщине по имени Elisabetha Mohren в 1747 г. в местности ганглофф, ландкрис кусел, беиирн, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он был похоронен в местности ганглофф, ландкрис кусел, беиирн, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Johann Jacob Nagel nació en 1728 como hijo de Jacob Nagel y Maria Margaretha. Se casó con Elisabetha Mohren en 1747, en Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Fue sepultado en Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Alemania.
